## Equations
### F001 — Lambda to lambda conversion

*Converts the paper perturbation parameter into the sampled Hamiltonian parameter.*

$$
lambda = sqrt(2 pi Lambda / d)
$$

status: `verified` · source: paper_formula: Lambda = lambda^2 d / (2 pi)

Numerical form:

```
lambda_from_Lambda(Lambda, dimension)
```

Code: `code/src/boson_sampling_chaos.py::lambda_from_Lambda`


### F002 — Random-matrix Hamiltonian normalization

*Defines integrable-to-chaotic one-particle dynamics.*

$$
H=(H_0+lambda V)/sqrt(1+lambda^2)
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `F001` · source: paper_formula: H=(H0+lambda V)/sqrt(1+lambda^2)

Numerical form:

```
sample_hamiltonian draws H0 and GOE V, then normalizes by sqrt(1+lambda^2).
```

Code: `code/src/boson_sampling_chaos.py::sample_hamiltonian`, `code/src/boson_sampling_chaos.py::sample_goe`


### F003 — Two-photon permanent probability

*Turns a one-particle unitary into collision-free two-photon output probabilities.*

$$
p(r,s)=|U_{r,i}U_{s,j}+U_{r,j}U_{s,i}|^2
$$

status: `verified` · source: paper_formula: p=|Per(U_sub)|^2

Numerical form:

```
conditional_two_photon_distribution computes the squared permanent for all output pairs.
```

Code: `code/src/boson_sampling_chaos.py::conditional_two_photon_distribution`


### F004 — Collision-free conditional normalization

*Matches the post-selected two-click distribution used by the paper figures.*

$$
p_cond(r,s)=p(r,s)/sum_{r<s} p(r,s)
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `F003` · source: paper_formula: p_cond=p/sum_collision_free p

Numerical form:

```
probabilities / probabilities.sum() over collision-free pairs.
```

Code: `code/src/boson_sampling_chaos.py::conditional_two_photon_distribution`


### F005 — Porter-Thomas dimension and reference distribution

*Defines the PT reference used to diagnose chaotic output statistics.*

$$
D=binom(M,2), P_PT(p)=D exp(-D p)
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `F004` · source: paper_formula: D=C(8,2)=28, P_PT(p)=D exp(-D p)

Numerical form:

```
porter_thomas_w1 compares sorted probabilities against PT quantiles.
```

Code: `code/src/boson_sampling_chaos.py::collision_free_pairs`, `code/src/boson_sampling_chaos.py::porter_thomas_w1`


### F006 — Shannon entropy and Haar reference

*Measures spreading of the two-photon probability vector.*

$$
S=-sum_i p_i log p_i
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `F004` · source: paper_formula: S=-sum p log p, S_Haar=-1+sum_i 1/i

Numerical form:

```
shannon_entropy(probabilities)
```

Code: `code/src/boson_sampling_chaos.py::shannon_entropy`


### F007 — OTOC short-time scaling

*Checks the short-time order of two-photon spreading.*

$$
C(t) ~ t^2 or t^4 depending on output-input overlap
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `F003`, `F004` · source: paper_feature: overlap-one outputs scale as t^2 and overlap-zero outputs as t^4

Numerical form:

```
ensemble_metrics groups probabilities by overlap_count and checks log-log slopes.
```

Code: `code/src/boson_sampling_chaos.py::overlap_count`, `code/src/boson_sampling_chaos.py::ensemble_metrics`


### F008 — Participation ratio

*Measures delocalization of the output probability vector.*

$$
PR=1/sum_i p_i^2
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `F004` · source: paper_formula: PR=1/sum_i p_i^2

Numerical form:

```
participation_ratio(probabilities)
```

Code: `code/src/boson_sampling_chaos.py::participation_ratio`


### F009 — Fourth-order spectral form factor proxy

*Provides the local spectral diagnostic plotted with PT distance and entropy.*

$$
SFF_4(t)=|Tr exp(-iHt)|^4/d^4
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `F002` · source: paper_feature: SFF diagnostic used with PT and entropy probes

Numerical form:

```
spectral_form_factor_4(eigenvalues, time)
```

Code: `code/src/boson_sampling_chaos.py::spectral_form_factor_4`
